issue_title: 如何检测 client连接 已建立 和 已断开
issue_body:
Hi

先说下我的测试流程

我在本地执行 sslocal 建立链接后 

```shell
$ sslocal -s xxx  -p 22001 -k xxx -m xxx -l 1081 -v
2016-08-25 18:30:38 INFO     loading libcrypto from libcrypto.so.1.0.0
2016-08-25 18:30:38 INFO     starting local at 127.0.0.1:1081
2016-08-25 18:30:38 DEBUG    using event model: epoll
```

然后我在服务器上查看端口情况发现并没有 ESTABLISHED 状态的链接

```shell
root@ubuntu-xenial:~# netstat -natp | grep ':22001'
tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:22001           0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      2575/ssserver
```

然后我在本地开浏览器访问网页后
再次到服务器上查看端口情况发现这个时候有 ESTABLISHED 状态的链接了

```shell
root@ubuntu-xenial:~# netstat -natp | grep ':22001'
tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:22001           0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      2575/ss-server  
tcp        0      0 192.168.99.21:22001     192.168.99.1:58776      ESTABLISHED 2575/ss-server  
tcp        0      0 192.168.99.21:22001     192.168.99.1:58848      ESTABLISHED 2575/ss-server 
```

当我等待了几十妙后，再次在服务器上查看端口情况，发现之前的链接状态都到了 TIME_WAIT 了

```shell
root@ubuntu-xenial:~# netstat -natp | grep ':22001'
tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:22001           0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      2575/ss-server  
tcp        0      0 192.168.99.21:22001     192.168.99.1:58776      TIME_WAIT   -               
tcp        0      0 192.168.99.21:22001     192.168.99.1:58848      TIME_WAIT   -               
```

再等段时间后，再次在服务器上查看端口情况，发现之前的链接都没有了

```shell
root@ubuntu-xenial:~# netstat -natp | grep ':22001'
tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:22001           0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      2575/ssserver
```

-------------------------------------

由上面的过程可以判断 sslocal 只有在有请求的时候才会建立链接，当没有请求的时候会释放链接

那么问题来了

问题1：在服务器端如何判断 某个端口 是否有 client 建立了链接呢？
通过检测端口是否 ESTABLISHED 有一定的问题，ssserver 的 manager udp 也没有提供对应的接口
我想到的方法是间接的通过 udp 的 ping 命令来判断 流量是否为0，如果大于0 就可以认为是建立过链接

问题2：在服务器端如何判断 某个端口 之前建立成功链接的 client 是否断开链接了（关闭了 client） 呢？
通过 udp 的 ping 命令判断 流量 长时间的固定在某个值？

上面两个问题大家怎么看？

issue_title: [v2.py] `./pants run` implementation can be problematic in the face of application re-execs
issue_body:
in the v2 python backend, the `run` task implementation now uses PEX_PATH to compose a pex environment at runtime.

we had a particular case in our monorepo where a user was attempting to use `./pants run` in conjunction with a development server that would re-exec itself when e.g. files changed on disk. in the v1 backend, this worked fine as everything was self-contained in a singular pex that could be neatly re-executed with it's full environmental context intact. in the v2 backend, this mode of re-execution now breaks with import errors because the PEX_PATH composition context is not self-contained (i.e. after a re-exec, it may be missing the entire sources pex environment).

seems like we'll need to figure out a strategy to make self-contained re-execs possible again - and potentially also a flag to enable `run` to run directly from sources vs a composed pex to support the "live development" workflow. cc @benjyw in case he has any ideas on either of these.

issue_title: make error messages in concurrent work in jvm compile execution_graph.py respect --print-exception-stacktrace
issue_body: If an error occurs while processing the result of e.g. a zinc compile, the error message will be shown alone, without a corresponding exception type, line number, or stacktrace. If we add `log.debug(traceback.format_exc())` at line 267 of `jvm_compile`'s `execution_graph.py`, then [if a zinc job(s) fails](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/130a937e8d4dbb3084775b7c627acf3f4b31c398/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/jvm_compile/execution_graph.py#L326), we get to see the entire stacktrace as well. This makes errors that bubble up from invoking the `work()` method much easier to diagnose and fix.

issue_title: Need a way to get paragraph outline level
issue_body:
Hi! First, thank you for your great library.

Some documents miss heading styles but still keep the sectining. Moreover, styles can be named in arbitrary manner, so there can be no chance to get document outline for some particular document.

Can we please provide a way to get outline elevel of a paragraph? It looks like Word stores it using `<w:outlineLvl w:val="...">`.

It is [mentioned once in the code](https://github.com/python-openxml/python-docx/blob/e784a73bfcdc24ba08f772055f8b4240f420adcf/docx/oxml/text/parfmt.py#L47), but I see no highlevel way to get it out.

issue_title: upgrade major client-side dependencies
issue_body: We may want to upgrade major dependencies (particularly Vue.js) to get any recent bug and performance improvements. Definitely something to do early - we shouldn't try to do this right before releasing.

issue_title: Minimum browser support
issue_body:
I'd like to concretize the browsers we're willing to support for Kolibri to the following:

- Internet Explorer 10+
- Firefox 35+
- Chrome 40+
- Android browser 4.2+
- iOS 6.0+
- Safari 5+
- ~~Opera~~ (latest ones use the same rendering engine as Chrome)

The versions given above are meant to be negotiated. But we need to commit to supporting certain versions soon as that will determine what external dependencies we'll be bundling in (see [Kolibri Frontend Architecture doc](https://trello.com/c/wlRWKYUe/13-front-end-architecture-high-level-concepts)).

To answer this realistically, we'll need to look at our most important partners and deployments, and see what technologies they're using. Here's a list of people we need to contact:

- FUNSEPA
- Nalanda
- WCH
- UNICEF
- Aflatoun
- ~~World Bank~~ (Premature to contact, according to Liz)
- {{ add more here @jamalex and @jeepurs }}

issue_title: TypeError: compile() takes exactly 2 arguments (3 given)
Create Custom Creative example:  createCreative is not a method
issue_body:
In [create_custom_creative example](https://github.com/googleads/googleads-python-lib/blob/master/examples/dfp/v201602/creative_service/create_custom_creative.py), the method createCreative does not exist according to the [API Reference](https://developers.google.com/doubleclick-publishers/docs/reference/v201602/CreativeService) (v201602).

```python
  # Call service to create the creative.
  creative = creative_service.createCreative(creative)

  # Display results.
  if creative:
    print ('Template creative with id \'%s\', name \'%s\', and type \'%s\' was '
           'created and can be previewed at %s.'
           % (creative['id'], creative['name'], dfp.DfpClassType(creative),
              creative['previewUrl']))
```

Must be:

```python
  # Call service to create the creative.
  creative = creative_service.createCreatives(creative)

  # Display results.
  for creative in creatives:
    print ('Template creative with id \'%s\', name \'%s\', and type \'%s\' was '
           'created and can be previewed at %s.'
           % (creative['id'], creative['name'], dfp.DfpClassType(creative),
              creative['previewUrl']))
```

issue_title: Default GMRES Inconsistent Between Code/Documentation
issue_body: Documentation for GMRES says that it defaults to Householder implementation.  But looking at source code for GMRES, it appears as if it is defaulting to Modified Gram-Schmidt.

issue_title: curl (or sbt) should be included in the nix default environment
issue_body:
When trying to build tensorframes (e.g. `./build/sbt distribution/compile`) within the default nix shell, sbt tries to download itself using curl, which fails as curl is not installed by default. 

Adding curl as a dependency in `default.nix` may fix this issue.

issue_body:

<!--- You can also use gist.github.com links for larger files -->

##### SUMMARY
<!--- Explain the problem briefly -->

This was working before. I adapted the conf file to the new syntax.

It starts running testcbbc.py but stops before the initial blacklist.conf file is processed. It never reaches the point that says Loop endlessly to allow ExaBGP to continue running. It never reaches the ins.close() statement.
When pressing CTRL-C, it does acknowlege the CTRL-C but never ends. It will never return to the shell prompt.
There are some 80K entries in the file; it stops after about 14K. The BGP session with the peer is initially established. The peer initially sees the prefixes but the session dies probably because of timeout.

with open("/etc/exabgp/ip-blacklist.conf", "r") as ins:

        with open("/etc/exabgp/ip-deltafile.conf", "r") as ins:
            for line in ins:
                if line.startswith('-'):
                    line2 = line.rstrip('\n')
                    line3 = line2.rstrip('\r')
                    line4 = line3.lstrip('-')
                    stdout.write( 'withdraw route ' + line4 + ' next-hop 192.0.2.1' + '\n')
                    stdout.flush()
                else:
                    line2 = line.rstrip('\n')
                    line3 = line2.rstrip('\r')
                    stdout.write( 'announce route ' + line3 + ' next-hop 192.0.2.1 community [65532:666]' + '\n')
                    stdout.flush()
        ins.close()
        call (["rm", "/etc/exabgp/ip-deltafile.conf"])
#


##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE
<!---
For bugs, please if possible, please run with the "-d" option and provide the full output,
If not possible, please provide the full fault section as reported by ExaBGP
-->

There is no fault, it just stops.

issue_title: TTY support on Docker services
issue_body: The Docker REST API supports a TTY parameter on /service/create, but the Python API does not. See [here](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.29/#operation/ServiceCreate). This was implemented in Moby [here](https://github.com/moby/moby/pull/28076).

issue_title: XBIFReader incompatible with SAMIAM export (and spec?)
issue_body:
The CPDs come in transposed - such that they are no longer valid, for example:

Z      |  Z_0  | Z_1 |           Z      |  Z_0  | Z_1 | 
X_0  |  .8    |   .4   |     =>  X_0  |  .8    |   .2   |
X_1  |  .2    |   .6   |           X_1  |  .4    |   .6   |

According to the spec at http://www.cs.cmu.edu/~fgcozman/Research/InterchangeFormat/ the tables should be read:

"taking the GIVEN variables first and then
the FOR variables (if all variables were binary, we would say binary
counting with least significant digit in the right)."

issue_title: Documentation should encourage restricing install to compatible versions
issue_body:
iirc the project is following semver.  The documentation should encourage the user to follow best practices, including helping them from accidentally upgrade to an incompatible version until they are ready

For example, instead of 
```bash
python -m pip install nidmm
```
have
```bash
python -m pip install nidmm~=0.6.0
```

According to [PEP 440](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#compatible-release), the compatible release version specifier will translate from `~=0.6.0` to `>=0.6.0,==0.6.*`

issue_title: Overriding environment variables causes them to be interpreted as a string
issue_body:

Michael Schmitz So there is one issue with using environment variables as fallback in HOCON. These environment variables are assumed to be strings by default which lead to incompatible type errors in AllenNLP. E.g. when setting cuda_device using environment variables

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/allennlp/training/trainer.py", line 515, in from_params
    if cuda_device >= 0:
TypeError: '>=' not supported between instances of 'str' and 'int'
```

I can submit a PR to allennlp to convert every parameter to the right type (or at least the interesting ones) or maybe there is a fancy way to ensure environment variables have the right type ?

issue_title: Submit jobs to queue locally
issue_body: There is currently no way to submit jobs to a queue locally although it is possible to submit jobs to a queue on a remote host. This functionality is important because a) for long running jobs it may be easier to have the parent job running on a remote server and b) it is often desirable to store the data remotely as well.

issue_title: HA PostgreSQL in K8S
issue_body:
Deis v2 will continue to use PostgreSQL. However, we want an HA version of Postgres with failover support, fault tolerance, and easy recovery.

Recovery data (e.g. WAL logs) should be stored in object storage (Ceph, Swift, S3, etc.)

We should run at least one master node, and at least one minion node, with dynamic failover.

Something that i've been missing a lot when moving from Dokku to Deis is the possibility of rebuilding apps: https://github.com/scottatron/dokku-rebuild

In Deis, i have to create empty commits just to bump the HEAD and trigger a rebuild. And this is very dirty.

issue_title: Best way to support multiple versions of the same app. (eg: DEV, QA, PROD)
issue_body:
Have a Deis cluster, and want to configure/release apps for different purposes. I believe I can use .env to set parameters that the app uses (eg: which database it talks to and which versions of other services)... but is there a good way to switch between them?  Is there a way to have three versions of app foo running?

eg: git push deis prod for prod, git push deis qa for qa (and have them pick up the appropriate environment?)

These are go apps so they live in the go path source directory (otherwise I could just have three copies of the git repo with different ENVs)

What's the best practice for this?

Yes, I know prod should probably be its own cluster and it will eventually but we will at least still need DEV and QA.

issue_title: Use
Keccak256 no longer picklable
issue_body: https://github.com/ethereum/eth-hash/commit/99d647e9832f12672c52556dbb21b4c38a75c35d makes `Keccak256` keep a reference to the backend module (e.g. `eth_hash.backends.pycryptodome`) whereas before it'd keep references to the backend's functions (`keccak256` and `preimage`). That is a problem because it means `Keccak256` is no longer picklable, which means we can't use it with, say, a `concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor`

issue_title: install of pip doesn't install wheel
issue_body: Just saw an interested talk at pycon au about conda. One thing that came up was that when pip is installed by conda into an environment, wheel isn't installed. Installing wheel is important to get the pip 7.0+ wheel caching feature, which is a significant performance boost.

issue_title: Disable .netrc authentication
issue_body:
Anaconda-Server/support#23

When a user has a `~/.netrc` file present, `requests` will use that file to populate the `Authorization` header (using HTTP Basic auth). When authorization fails, conda falls into an infinite loop retrying.

To fix this problem in `anaconda-client`, I disabled `.netrc` authorization by adding a `NullAuth` to the `requests.Session`.

https://github.com/Anaconda-Server/anaconda-client/pull/298

issue_title: Memory usage with conda installation (on Travis)
issue_body:
Dear all,

We are using Travis for continuous integration. Our code base depends on a number of Python and R/Bioconductor packages that I am installing with Conda.

As you may know, Travis builds for open-source projects come with resource restrictions:
http://docs.travis-ci.com/user/ci-environment/#The-Build-Machine

namely, 3GB RAM + 1.5GB swap with 2 cores.

My problem here is with RAM usage. Our Travis builds fail due to the target system running out of memory and after some troubleshooting I have realised that the command:
```
conda create -n env pkg1 pkg2 etc.
```

consumes most of the RAM but in a non-deterministic fashion.

In a failed Travis build the amount of RAM, as reported by `free`, goes from 1.2GB to 4.5GB (maximum allowed by Travis). Please see before `conda create`:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262464#L124

and after:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262464#L571

This is the error reported by Travis:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262464#L682

In a successful Travis build the amount of RAM goes from 1.2GB to 1.8GB. Please see before `conda create`:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262465#L124

and after:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262465#L571

I have also found this thread:
https://github.com/travis-ci/travis-ci/issues/2904

kindly asking for higher amount of resources for Travis builds but that is not going to happen soon.

I have used `conda clean --index-cache` with no success. I was wondering whether you could provide advice on this or do you think I need to ask to Travis forum instead?

PS: My Conda recipes for R/Bioconductor packages are not uploaded to Git Hub yet but I am willing to do so soon, just in case anyone is interested in having them.

issue_title: te idp
Conda-install clobbers bin scripts of the same name from different packages
issue_body:
Two different conda packages each contain separate scripts which get installed into a fresh conda environment's `bin` location. Since these separate scripts happen to share the same filename, `conda install` silently overwrites the script from the first package with the script from the second package.

This is a problem because users have no way of knowing which of their packages got overwritten after the fact, and it can cause unexpected breakage.

The same problem exists for `conda update`.

issue_title: `conda.install.rm_rf` can't delete old environments in Windows + Python 2.7
issue_body:
This issue hit me when trying to use `--force` option in `conda env create` (I added this in #102), and it revealed a possible problem with how conda handles links in Windows + Python 2.7.

# Symptom

Trying to delete an environment (not active) from within Python fails because the original file being linked is locked by Windows.

# Context

* Windows
* Python 2.7 (`os.islink` does not work here, which might affect the `rm_rf` function from `conda.install`)

# Reproducing

This command line is enough to reproduce the problem:

```bat
$ conda create -n test pyyaml && python -c "import yaml;from conda.install import rm_rf;rm_rf('C:\\Miniconda\\envs\\test')"
Fetching package metadata: ......

#
# To activate this environment, use:
# > activate test
#

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "C:\Miniconda\lib\site-packages\conda\install.py", line 204, in rm_rf
    shutil.rmtree(path)
  File "C:\Miniconda\lib\shutil.py", line 247, in rmtree
    rmtree(fullname, ignore_errors, onerror)
  File "C:\Miniconda\lib\shutil.py", line 247, in rmtree
    rmtree(fullname, ignore_errors, onerror)
  File "C:\Miniconda\lib\shutil.py", line 252, in rmtree
    onerror(os.remove, fullname, sys.exc_info())
  File "C:\Miniconda\lib\shutil.py", line 250, in rmtree
    os.remove(fullname)
WindowsError: [Error 5] Acesso negado: 'C:\\Miniconda\\envs\\test\\Lib\\site-packages\\yaml.dll'
```

Note that when I import `pyyaml`, I'm locking file `C:\\Miniconda\\Lib\\site-packages\\yaml.dll` (root environment), but that also prevents me from deleting `C:\\Miniconda\\envs\\test\\Lib\\site-packages\\yaml.dll` (another hard link).

# Solution?

Maybe we should add some improved support for detecting and unlinking hardlinks in Windows with Python 2.7, and handle those cases individually instead of trying to `shutil.rmtree` everything.

Possibly from [jarako.windows](https://bitbucket.org/jaraco/jaraco.windows/src/default/jaraco/windows/filesystem/__init__.py#cl-76) or [ntfs/fs.py](https://github.com/sid0/ntfs/blob/master/ntfsutils/fs.py#L88)

issue_title: [ACR] Design discussion for az acr repository command group
issue_body:
### Description

`az acr repository` contains a few commands to delete a repository from a container registry or delete a manifest/tag from a repository in a container registry. Due to the complex dependency nature among docker manifest/tag/layer, using this command group becomes quite confusing and complicated.

Current implementation:
```
Command
    az acr repository delete: Deletes a repository or a manifest/tag from the given repository in
    the specified container registry.

Arguments
    --name -n    [Required]: The name of the container registry. You can configure the default
                             registry name using `az configure --defaults acr=<registry name>`.
    --repository [Required]: The name of repository to delete.
    --manifest             : The sha256 based digest of manifest to delete.
    --password -p          : The password used to log into a container registry.
    --resource-group -g    : Name of resource group. You can configure the default group using `az
                             configure --defaults group=<name>`.
    --tag                  : The name of tag to delete.
    --username -u          : The username used to log into a container registry.
    --yes -y               : Do not prompt for confirmation.

Examples
    Delete a repository from the specified container registry.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository

    Delete a tag from the given repository. This operation does not delete the manifest referenced
    by the tag or associated layer data.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository --tag MyTag

    Delete the manifest referenced by a tag. This operation also deletes associated layer data and
    all other tags referencing the manifest.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository --tag MyTag --manifest

    Delete a manfiest from the given repository. This operation also deletes associated layer data
    and all tags referencing the manifest.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository --manifest MyManifest
```

## Proposed implementation

Assuming `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7` is the manifest digest for the image `hello-world:latest`.

### Delete image by manifest digest
```
az acr repository delete-image --name MyRegistry --image hello-world@sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7
```
Ask for confirmation, this will delete the manifest `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7` from repository `hello-world` and all tags referencing it. This will further result in automatic layer data purging at a future time.

### Delete image by tag
```
az acr repository delete-image --name MyRegistry --image hello-world:latest
```
This will delete the tag `latest` from repository `hello-world`. If there is at least one other tag referencing the manifest `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7`, this command is the same as the below one to simply delete a tag. Otherwise, the manifest will also be deleted and will result in automatic layer data purging at a future time. This is basically the similar behavior as `docker rmi` command.

### Delete a tag
```
az acr repository delete-tag --name MyRegistry --image hello-world:latest
```
This will simply delete the tag `latest` from repository `hello-world`. The image `hello-world:latest` can no longer be pulled, but the manifest `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7` and all associated layer data still exist.

### Delete a repository
```
az acr repository delete --name MyRegistry --repository hello-world
```
The same as the current implementation. All tags, manifests, and layer data will be deleted.

Any suggestions are appreciated.

/cc @SajayAntony @sivagms @SteveLasker

issue_title: [ACR] Design discussion for az acr repository command group
issue_body:
### Description

`az acr repository` contains a few commands to delete a repository from a container registry or delete a manifest/tag from a repository in a container registry. Due to the complex dependency nature among docker manifest/tag/layer, using this command group becomes quite confusing and complicated.

Current implementation:
```
Command
    az acr repository delete: Deletes a repository or a manifest/tag from the given repository in
    the specified container registry.

Arguments
    --name -n    [Required]: The name of the container registry. You can configure the default
                             registry name using `az configure --defaults acr=<registry name>`.
    --repository [Required]: The name of repository to delete.
    --manifest             : The sha256 based digest of manifest to delete.
    --password -p          : The password used to log into a container registry.
    --resource-group -g    : Name of resource group. You can configure the default group using `az
                             configure --defaults group=<name>`.
    --tag                  : The name of tag to delete.
    --username -u          : The username used to log into a container registry.
    --yes -y               : Do not prompt for confirmation.

Examples
    Delete a repository from the specified container registry.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository

    Delete a tag from the given repository. This operation does not delete the manifest referenced
    by the tag or associated layer data.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository --tag MyTag

    Delete the manifest referenced by a tag. This operation also deletes associated layer data and
    all other tags referencing the manifest.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository --tag MyTag --manifest

    Delete a manfiest from the given repository. This operation also deletes associated layer data
    and all tags referencing the manifest.
        az acr repository delete -n MyRegistry --repository MyRepository --manifest MyManifest
```

## Proposed implementation

Assuming `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7` is the manifest digest for the image `hello-world:latest`.

### Delete image by manifest digest
```
az acr repository delete --name MyRegistry --image hello-world@sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7
```
Ask for confirmation, this will delete the manifest `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7` from repository `hello-world` and all tags referencing it. This will further result in automatic layer data purging.

### Delete image by tag
```
az acr repository delete --name MyRegistry --image hello-world:latest
```
Ask for confirmation, this will delete the manifest that is referenced by tag `latest` (e.g., `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7`) from repository `hello-world` and all tags referencing it. This will further result in automatic layer data purging.

### Delete a tag
```
az acr repository untag --name MyRegistry --image hello-world:latest
```
This will simply delete the tag `latest` from repository `hello-world`. The image `hello-world:latest` can no longer be pulled, but the manifest `sha256:c5515758d4c5e1e838e9cd307f6c6a0d620b5e07e6f927b07d05f6d12a1ac8d7` and all associated layer data still exist.

### Delete a repository
```
az acr repository delete --name MyRegistry --repository hello-world
```
The same as the current implementation. All tags, manifests, and layer data will be deleted.

Any suggestions are appreciated.

/cc @SajayAntony @sivagms @SteveLasker

issue_title: Conversion to managed drives renames the drives
issue_body:
The errors started happening after the machines were converted from unmanaged to managed drives.
Example:
Message: The Resource 'Microsoft.Compute/disks/string000-OSDisk' under resource group 'xxxx' was not found.
Failed to create snapshot of: string000-OSDisk
Azure Error: ResourceNotFound
Message: The Resource 'Microsoft.Compute/disks/string000-LogDisk' under resource group 'xxxx' was not found.
Failed to create snapshot of: string000-LogDisk
Azure Error: ResourceNotFound
Message: The Resource 'Microsoft.Compute/disks/string000-DataDisk' under resource group 'xxxx' was not found.
Failed to create snapshot of: string000-DataDisk
Azure Error: ResourceNotFound

Both azure portal and python SDK show the disks named string000-DataDisk, string000-OSDisk, string000-LogDisk are attached to the machine string000. Disks are functional and we can see the data on our mountpoints.

However, when I rebuild the machine with the above disk names they appear empty. They only work as expected if left alone after the conversion.

I also noticed that the disks attached to some of the machines in this resource group after the conversion are named:
string001-OSDisk_random hex string
string001-LogDisk_random hex string
string001-DataDisk_random hex string
They were originally created as unmanaged without the hex string.

This makes me suspect that the disks attached to string000 have the hex string appended to them, but some APIs are not aware of it. There are no references to disk renaming or how the conversion is done under the hood in the documentation.

Disks are P30 and the errors seem reproducible only when there is a significant amount of data on them.
I was able to reproduce in multiple resource groups, but not on every run.

Is there another way to check for the "real" name of the disks attached to string000?

Thanks!

issue_title: States relabeling
issue_body:
A small practical addition would be a way to reorder the states of a HMM, e.g. `model.relabel_states(idxs)` where `idxs` is some permutation of `range(n_states)`.  This should reorder the entries in `transmat_`, `startprob_`, `means_`, etc. accordingly.

A typical use case would be to sort the states after a B-W fit, e.g. by lifetime, or by mean in the case of a 1D model (e.g., `model.relabel_states(np.argsort(model.means_.squeeze()))`).

issue_title: Jupyterhub formgrader returning error 404
issue_body: I am trying to deploy jupyterhub with nbgrader for teaching, running within a docker container. I am using github oauth through oauthenticator. Everything seems to be working except formgrader. I think that when formgrader starts as a managed service, it is unable to find nbgrader_config.py. I say this because if I change the formgrader port in nbgrader_config.py, it has no effect on the port used when formgrader is started. What do I need to do to ensure that nbgrader_config can be found? Thank you for your help.

issue_title: Body css setting, why absolute?
issue_body:
Not sure if this is the right place to post it, please correct me if I post it wrong.

I'm developing a sidebar for Ipython Notebook (https://github.com/ipython-contrib/IPython-notebook-extensions/issues/532):
![toc](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2425141/13720167/a7f8a058-e83c-11e5-8e84-4ebda0c660dc.gif)

I want to use **Twitter Bootstrap**'s scrollspy (http://getbootstrap.com/javascript/#scrollspy), but it requires the `body` css `position:relative`. And I took a look at the code, it isn't. 

I'm wondering why set `body` to `position:absolute`? And is it possible to change it?

issue_title: guidance ref programmatically adding cell metadata / manually adding on classic nb works
iPython with Django
issue_body:
I'm new to jupyter/ipython and django. I have Django 1.9.5 installed on Ubuntu 16.04 and my application is running fine. I want to configure iPython(version 5.1.0) to be used with my Django application. Basically trying to call the iPython notebook (browser) from django which later can be used extensively in the application through a user interface.

When I try to run the command below

$./manage.py shell_plus --notebook

I get an error:

[W 13:46:39.247 NotebookApp] Unrecognized JSON config file version, assuming version 1
[I 13:46:41.082 NotebookApp] [nb_conda_kernels] enabled, 2 kernels found
[I 13:46:41.114 NotebookApp] Writing notebook server cookie secret to /run/user/1000/jupyter/notebook_cookie_secret
[I 13:46:41.399 NotebookApp] ✓ nbpresent HTML export ENABLED
[W 13:46:41.399 NotebookApp] ✗ nbpresent PDF export DISABLED: No module named nbbrowserpdf.exporters.pdf
[I 13:46:41.409 NotebookApp] [nb_conda] enabled
[I 13:46:41.507 NotebookApp] [nb_anacondacloud] enabled
**CommandError: No notebook (Python) kernel specs found**

To resolve this I tried: ipython2 kernelspec install-self --user

Under home directory .local/share/jupyter/kernels folder there is a kernel.json file

I'm not sure why I'm still getting the same error. Any suggestion will be very helpful in resolving this issue. Thank you.

To achieve launching iPython notebook from Django I tried the following:

>>Installed the django-extensions: pip install django-extensions
>>In django settings.py file added the line django_extensions to INSTALLED_APPS
>>Ran ipython profile create and uncommented this line :  c.InteractiveShellApp.extensions = ['django_notebook']

issue_title: Test passing but no coverage
issue_body:
During testing of a function ( this fucntion takes a `Pdb` object name `dbg` and call the method runcall to debug any external function --> 'fucn', `debug_mode_function` is created to debug the generators)

```python
def debug_mode_function(func, dbg, *args, **kwargs):
    results = []
    bear_results = dbg.runcall(func, *args, **kwargs)
    if isinstance(bear_results, collections.Iterable):
        try:
            iterator = iter(bear_results)
            while True:
                result = dbg.runcall(next, iterator)
                results.append(result)
        except StopIteration:
            return results
    else:
        return results
```
So to test it I have created a test using `StringIO` as I am caputing the value which I will get and after that asserting the string value 

```python
def test_debug_mode_function(self):
    args = {}
    kwargs = {}
    def fun(*args, **kwargs):
        yield 1
        yield 2
    input = StringIO('q\nq\nq')
    output = StringIO()
    dbg = Pdb(stdin=input ,stdout=output)
    dbg.do_q = dbg.do_continue
    self.assertEqual(debug_mode_function(fun, dbg, *args, **kwargs), [1,2])
    output = output.getvalue()
    self.assertEqual(output.split('\n')[1], '-> yield 1')
    self.assertEqual(output.split('\n')[3], '-> yield 2')
    output.close()
```

issue_title: With Tomcat, error 20  unable to get local issuer certificate even after fullchain.pem
issue_body:
We have a Debian 7 X64 server running Apache Tomcat on which I am trying to install a certificate from LetsEncrypt. I am partially successful as I am getting an error where certificate verification is failing.
I followed the guide on letsencrypts community site [https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/how-to-use-the-certificate-for-tomcat/3677/25
](url)
For adding LetsEncrypt to Tomcat, I had to create a new JKS and add the converted keys into the JKS keystore, which is referred in Apache Tomcat. This are the steps I followed:

Commands:

```

issue_title: No alphabetical sort for triggers of same type and number of words
issue_body:
According to the [sorting of triggers](https://www.rivescript.com/wd/RiveScript.html#Sorting-Triggers), 

> For multiple triggers with the same amount of words, sort them by length, and then alphabetically if there are still matches in length. 

However, it seems that the sort by character-length and alphabetical order are not yet implemented. 

An example of set of triggers, this can be evaluated from [Try RiveScript Online](https://www.rivescript.com/try):
```
+ * you *
- you_reply
+ * are *
- are_reply
```
Both of them matches the user input `how are you doing` but the RiveScript answer will be `you_reply`, the first occurrence in the `.rive` file (because the triggers are stored as list). Change the order of triggers and you will find it matches the first occurrence. The dependence on ordering of templates in such cases violates the sorting rule stated earlier (in alphabetical order).  

Another example of atomic triggers which requires activation of the RS debug mode by setting `rs = RiveScript(debug = True)` to see the sort order of triggers:
```
+ hi
- hi there
+ hello
- hello there
```
The sort order should be: `hello` then `hi` because:

1. Length in terms of characters (`len("hello")=5`, `len("hi")=2`)

2. Alphabetical order. 

However, the order search information shows that sorted list of triggers places `hi` before `hello`
```
You> hello
[RS] Get reply to [localuser] hello
[RS] Checking topic random for any %Previous's.
[RS] There is a %Previous in this topic!
[RS] lastReply: undefined
[RS] Try to match u'hello' against u'hi' (u'^hi$')
[RS] Try to match u'hello' against u'hello' (u'^hello$')
[RS] Found a match!
[RS] Reply: hello there
Bot> hello there
```

issue_body: After load model, we need to modify last layers of pre-trained model to fine tune with different dataset. Another use case is that we need to remove last layers( i.e. Fully Connected layer, etc.) to only extract features without doing classification.

issue_title: Prediction Results Do Not Match Original XGBoost
issue_body:
I trained a model with a single iteration:
```python
param = {
    'nthread': 10,
    'objective': 'multi:softprob',
    'eval_metric': 'mlogloss',
    'num_class': 3,
    'silent': 1,
    'max_depth': 5,
    'min_child_weight': 5,
    'eta': 0.5,  # learning rate
    'subsample': 1,
    'colsample_bytree': 1,
    'gamma': 0, 
    'alpha': 0,
    'lambda': 1, 
}
watchlist  = [(dtest, 'eval')]
bst = xgb.train(param, dtrain, 1, watchlist)
```

`sample` will be a numpy array with a single row of features. The output of:

```python
bst.predict(xgb.DMatrix(sample))
```

differs noticeably from the output of:
```python
bst_lite = treelite.Model.from_xgboost(bst)
bst_lite.export_lib(toolchain='gcc', libpath=model_path, verbose=True)
batch = Batch.from_npy2d(sample)
predictor = Predictor(model_path, verbose=True)
predictor.predict(batch)
```

The problem is compounded when more trees are added.

Any ideas of what could be going on here?

Thanks for your work on this @hcho3 .

issue_title: Multiplication and `Real`
issue_body:
Hi,

the below file causes `z3` (v. 4.4.1)  to hang (as far as I can tell). I'm guessing its because
we're using `Int` values `n` on a function declared for `Real` ? (It works fine if I change the 
sort of `Z3_OP_MUL` to `(x Int) (y Int) Int`. However, note that it also works fine with:

1. version `4.3.2` 
2. if I perturb the formulas a bit (i.e. we've been using this same formulation without trouble on other similar queries.

Just thought I'd point out!

Thanks!

Ranjit.


```
(set-option :auto-config false)
(set-option :model true)
(set-option :model.partial false)
(set-option :smt.mbqi false)

(define-fun Z3_OP_MUL ((x Real) (y Real)) Real (* x y))
(declare-fun ys () Int)
(declare-fun len (Int) Int)
(declare-fun xs () Int)
(declare-fun n () Int)
(declare-fun v () Int)
(declare-fun lq () Int)
(push 1)
(assert (and (= v lq)
             (>= (len xs) 0)
             (= n (len ys))
             (= (len lq) (len ys))
             ))
(push 1)
(assert (not (= (len v) (Z3_OP_MUL n n))))
(check-sat)
; SMT Says: Sat
(pop 1)
```

issue_title: Am I misunderstanding arrays or is this a bug?
issue_body:
Consider the following SMT-Lib script:

http://rise4fun.com/Z3/LuRm4

    (declare-const x Int)
    (declare-const a1 (Array Int Int))
    (assert (= (select a1 0) 0))
    (assert (= (select a1 1) 1))
    (assert (= (select a1 2) 2))
    (assert (= (select a1 0) 2))
    (check-sat)

This is an unsatisfactory model as we are asserting that index 0 of a1 is both 0 and 2. This is expected.

Consider the following:

http://rise4fun.com/Z3/njxqM

    (declare-const x Int)
    (declare-const a1 (Array Int Int))
    (assert (= (select a1 x) x))
    (assert (= (select a1 2) 0))
    (check-sat)

This is a satisfactory model - why? We are asserting that an element at index x is equal to x - and as x has no conditions tied to it, we are essentially creating an array such as: [0] = 0, [1] = 1, [2] = 2 etc... But at the same time we are also asserting that element 2 is equal to 0.

Am I misunderstanding arrays or is this a bug? If I am misunderstanding them, are there any resources which may help me? The Z3 guide on rise4fun is a little sparse on these subjects, and the formal documentation on the ArraysEx theory is well over my head!

issue_title: Subdirectory with the same name as the parent doesn't appear in listdir()
issue_body:
Given a directory structure
```
bucket/dir/dir
```
doing
```
from fs import open_fs
s3fs = open_fs('s3://bucket')
s3fs.listdir('dir')
```
returns the empty list. I reckon this is because of line 440 in `_s3fs.py`:
```
~/miniconda3/envs/myenv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/fs_s3fs/_s3fs.py in listdir(self, path)
    438                     _prefix = prefix.get('Prefix')
    439                     _name = _prefix[prefix_len:]
--> 440                     if _name != _s3_key:
    441                         _directory.append(_name.rstrip(self.delimiter))
    442                 for obj in result.get('Contents', ()):
```
In this example, `_s3_key == _name == 'dir'` so the subdirectory isn't included in the list of `_directories` :grimacing:

issue_title: Remove additional load after flushing an object to disk
issue_body:
When an object is flushed to disk the object is then immediately loaded back into memory and overloads itself.

This is an expensive operation and I'm not seeing a very good motivation for this to being performed on every single flush so I'm opting to have this removed.

issue_title: Handling missing fields
issue_body:
If you try to filter for fields whose value is the empty string, it also matches records without those fields:

```
> http 'https://kinto.dev.mozaws.net/v1/buckets/46524be8-0ad7-3ac6-e260-71f8993feffa/collections/tasks/records?any_arbitrary_field=""' -a user:pass
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Next-Page, Pragma, Retry-After, Backoff, Alert, Cache-Control, ETag, Last-Modified, Expires, Total-Records, Content-Length
Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store
Connection: keep-alive
Content-Length: 429
Content-Type: application/json
Date: Tue, 06 Jun 2017 20:50:47 GMT
ETag: "1496685126658"
Last-Modified: Mon, 05 Jun 2017 17:52:06 GMT
Server: nginx
Total-Records: 4
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

{
    "data": [
        {
            "done": false,
            "id": "3dd095cb-f4f7-4c87-8112-6cd3e7660f41",
            "last_modified": 1496685088616,
            "title": "test"
        },
        {
            "done": false,
            "id": "0717608f-1cf2-472b-8a49-3136d2811946",
            "last_modified": 1496685088608,
            "title": "blbl"
        },
        {
            "done": false,
            "id": "65e5b714-dcb2-4136-836c-996e83514297",
            "last_modified": 1496524477816,
            "title": "to a poo"
        },
        {
            "done": false,
            "id": "5a1fc9a7-6c6a-428e-b932-930a69d6495a",
            "last_modified": 1496422987261,
            "title": "444"
        }
    ]
}
```

This is because of https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/master/kinto/core/storage/postgresql/__init__.py#L709. Postgres appears to be the reference implementation of Kinto's storage backend (see #815), so you might expect this behavior to leak into other backends too.

The [documentation](https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/master/docs/api/1.x/filtering.rst) is pretty scanty on the subject of missing fields, saying only `Will return an error if a field is unknown`, and I think that's meant to only apply to `like_`.

I'm not 100% sure what we should expect this to do (much less how to implement it), but it seems like you'd expect missing fields to compare as different from everything, including null (see #1215), and not match `ILIKE`. In the tests, for instance, there's a couple tests that filter for `deleted != true` (which is intended to match "live" records) or `deleted == false` (which is intended not to match any records).

issue_title: Consolidate compact conn format
rsync distributor without force_full incorrectly skips publishing some units
issue_body:
Using a yum repo as an example, here's a sequence of events which demonstrates the problem:

1. Create a yum repository, and populate it with some content.
2. Publish the repository with a yum distributor.
3. Add additional content to the repository.
4. Publish the repository with an rsync distributor. (This publish will not include the new unit, since a yum publish hasn't happened for the unit.)
5. Publish the repository with a yum distributor.
6. Publish the repository with an rsync distributor. (This publish should include the new unit, since a yum publish has happened for the unit.)

For more information see: https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2532

issue_title: Why does ScaledFloatFrame wrapper gives HUGE boost in convergence speed?
issue_body:
I compared two DQN algorithms implemented with and without the ScaledFloatFrame wrapper used. It turns out that the one that uses the  ScaledFloatFrame wrapper converges almost 5 to 10 times faster than the one that doesn't use it. Using the BreakoutNoFrameSkip-v4 environment,  the one uses ScaledFloatFrame hits an average score of 10 per episode (5 lives per episode.) at 100,000 frames while that one that doesn't use it barely get 1 per episode. 

From what I've understand the ScaledFloatFrame only converts the input information from the 0-255 range 8-bit integers to a 0-1 32-bit float. I just cannot see how come this conversion can make such a big difference. I'm wondering if anyone can help me understand the mechanism behind it? Thank you!

I ran the code baselines.her.experiment.train for five different seeds and generated plots with some minor changes to the plotting code (see issue #311) for HandManipulateBlockRotateXYZ-v0 across 200 epochs. I verified from the .json file that the 'scope' was 'DDPG' and that the rewards were sparse. 

Unfortunately the performance of the algorithm was substantially worse than I found in the paper (above are my results and below are the results from Plappert et al., 2018):

![ahilan_handmanipulateblockrotatexyz-v0](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9452627/36922897-64fcacc4-1e61-11e8-9961-f64a944bdea3.png)
![plappert_handmanipulateblockrotatexyz](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9452627/36922898-65175182-1e61-11e8-9e43-49ce522a9b88.png)

Any idea why the results are so much worse? Further, this issue seems to apply to multiple other cases including 'FetchSlide' and 'HandManipulateBlockRotateZ' although I have only run one seed for these.

In case its useful, I also copy the details of the 'params.json' file:

{"pi_lr": 0.001, "network_class": "baselines.her.actor_critic:ActorCritic", "norm_clip": 5, "polyak": 0.95, "scope": "ddpg", "n_cycles": 50, "random_eps": 0.3, "env_name": "HandManipulateBlockRotateXYZ-v0", "rollout_batch_size": 2, "n_batches": 40, "layers": 3, "buffer_size": 1000000, "action_l2": 1.0, "Q_lr": 0.001, "clip_obs": 200.0, "hidden": 256, "test_with_polyak": false, "batch_size": 256, "noise_eps": 0.2, "n_test_rollouts": 10, "relative_goals": false, "norm_eps": 0.01, "max_u": 1.0, "replay_strategy": "future", "replay_k": 4}

And of course, I'd like to thank OpenAI for making their code and environments available; it's really helpful to independent researchers!

issue_title: Organization Change
issue_body:
Hey everyone, after having a great discussion with @pbchase, I've decided to move PyCap to a new organization. I'd like it to be a place where people can put interesting projects based against REDCap. I actually don't want it to be python focused ( :smile:  @wibeasley) but rather language agnostic. 

Obviously some projects have certain ownership that must be maintained, either at the laboratory or university level. I'm not asking for private code but rather fun projects that have either scratched an itch or you depend on day in and day out that should be shepherded by a group of like-minded individuals.

I just need a name! Thoughts?

I will definitely put everyone who has contributed either in code or otherwise.

@dckc @aaron0browne @tjrivera @nicholsn @bud42 @byvernault 

## To do list

- [ ] New ReadTheDocs project
- [ ] Update TravisCI (since github forwards org changes maybe that'll just work?)

issue_title: Django querysets default to unfiltered when empty
Ensure dev packages contain "dev" version
issue_body:
We update the version with the "dev" indicator after publishing a release, but we sometimes forget to do so in a timely manner. Other PRs get merged, before the "dev" indicator is in place. This doesn't really impact any actual release, but it does lead to misnamed/misordered packages in testpypi:

* https://test.pypi.org/project/watchmaker/#history

We ought to be able to avoid this by changing how we add the "build" to the version, in .travis.yml. I.e. also inject the "dev" indicator at the same time.

* https://github.com/plus3it/watchmaker/blob/develop/.travis.yml#L68

issue_body:
Hi,

What I am trying to do is to use lstm on IMDB data for sentiment analysis by updating the code in following link https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/examples/nlp/lstm.py

However it uses a preprocessed data, but I wanna use my own IMDB raw data (downloaded from http://ai.stanford.edu/~amaas/data/sentiment/)

Here is the code that I updated for the sentiment analysis, all the intermediate steps seem correct but the accuracy is not stable (as you can see results below). When I print the predictions at the end, I see that the probabilities for each class is very very close (like _[[0.4999946355819702, 0.5000053644180298], [0.5000001192092896, 0.49999988079071045], [0.49999362230300903, 0.5000064373016357], [0.49999985098838806, 0.5000001192092896]])_.

I don't think that the problem is overfitting, since when I try to predict train data again, the result is as above. I think I am missing some point or doing something wrong.

Any help is appreciated, Thanks

```
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from __future__ import division, print_function, absolute_import

allWords = []
allDocuments = []
allLabels = []

def readFile(fileName, allWords):


    file = codecs.open(fileName, encoding='utf-8')

    for line in file:
        line = line.lower().encode('utf-8')
        words = line.split()
        for word in words:
            word = word.translate(None, string.punctuation)
            if word != '':
                allWords.append(word)

    file.close()

    file = codecs.open(fileName, encoding='utf-8')
    document = []

        line = line.lower().encode('utf-8')
        words = line.split()
        for word in words:
            word = word.translate(None, string.punctuation)
            if word in dictionary:
                index = dictionary[word]
            else:
                index = 0  # dictionary['UNK'] 
            document.append(index)
        allDocuments.append(document)
        allLabels.append(label)

    file.close()


vocabulary_size = 10000

def build_dataset(words):
  count = [['UNK', -1]]

    dictionary[word] = len(dictionary)
  data = list()

      index = dictionary[word]
    else:
      index = 0  # dictionary['UNK']
      unk_count = unk_count + 1
    data.append(index)
  count[0][1] = unk_count
  reverse_dictionary = dict(zip(dictionary.values(), dictionary.keys())) 
  return dictionary, reverse_dictionary



fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/train/neg/*.txt")
for file in fileList:
    readFile(file, allWords)

fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/test/train/*.txt")
for file in fileList:

print(len(allWords))

dictionary, reverse_dictionary = build_dataset(allWords)
del allWords  # Hint to reduce memory.

print(len(dictionary))

fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/train/neg/*.txt")
for file in fileList:
    readFileToConvertWordsToIntegers(dictionary, file, allDocuments, allLabels, 0)

fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/train/pos/*.txt")
for file in fileList:
    readFileToConvertWordsToIntegers(dictionary, file, allDocuments, allLabels, 1)

print(len(allDocuments))
print(len(allLabels))

c = list(zip(allDocuments, allLabels)) # shuffle them partitioning

random.shuffle(c)

allDocuments, allLabels = zip(*c)

trainX = allDocuments[:22500]
testX = allDocuments[22500:]

trainY = allLabels[:22500]
testY = allLabels[22500:]


#counter=collections.Counter(trainY)
#print(counter)


trainY = to_categorical(trainY, nb_classes=2)
testY = to_categorical(testY, nb_classes=2)

trainX = pad_sequences(trainX, maxlen=100, value=0.)
testX = pad_sequences(testX, maxlen=100, value=0.)
# Converting labels to binary vectors
trainY = to_categorical(trainY, nb_classes=2)
testY = to_categorical(testY, nb_classes=2)

# Network building
net = tflearn.input_data([None, 100])
net = tflearn.embedding(net, input_dim=vocabulary_size, output_dim=128)
net = tflearn.lstm(net, 128, dropout=0.8)
net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 2, activation='softmax')
net = tflearn.regression(net, optimizer='adam', learning_rate=0.001,
                         loss='categorical_crossentropy')

# Training
model = tflearn.DNN(net, tensorboard_verbose=0)
model.fit(trainX, trainY, validation_set=(testX, testY), show_metric=True,
          batch_size=32)
predictions = model.predict(trainX)
print(predictions)

issue_title: csvlook: Data inference should be off by default
issue_body:
Getting excel mangled data is a common problem e.g. data parsed as a date. I really would prefer not to see the same thing with a powerful tool like csvlook. For example this field '9.3,9.3' was interpreted as '2001-09-03'.

As the name suggests I want to look at the data, I don't want it to be parsed or interpreted. Please can you consider making data inference opt-in rather than opt-out?
Thanks.

issue_title: asynchronous messages blocking until a key is pressed
issue_body: I'm having an issue printing asynchronous messages while using cmdloop(). I have a CLI, and a threaded server that should be able to print messages as it receives them. As soon as any key is pressed, the expected messages are printed.

issue_body:
Hi,

thanks for building such a great tool. I used R and Rcpp for a while and I loved it, so I am looking for a python equivalent. I gave up with Python.Boost because I didn't manage to setup boost under windows. 

 I tried to follow the first steps from the guide in https://pybind11.readthedocs.io/en/latest/basics.html, but I encountered some problems. I am using Windows 7 with Visual Studio 2015 and Python 3.5.

I am not a python guru and pretty bad at compiling things, so it is perfectly possible that the problems I encountered are just a reflection of that.

The first issue is that the first steps do not seem to be really the first. Do I have to install pybind11 in some way before compiling the test cases? If yes how? with pip? with python stetup.py install? At the very least I have to download something, maybe this could be clarified under "first steps".  The docs say "Before getting started, make sure that development environment is set up to compile the included set of test cases.", but I don't understand what "set up to compile" exactly means. I assumed that I just had to download the latest pybind release from github, uncompress it, and create a build directory from the root of the git repository. I downloaded release 1.8.1.

    Python config failure: Python is 64-bit, chosen compiler is 32-bit.

issue_title: certbot-auto is not cleaning up temporary directory
issue_body:
The certbot-auto (letsencrypt-auto) script leaves behind a temporary directory every time it runs.  This looks like a regression as of release v0.5.0 (commit 4d4cfb414f2847f1814cfe2949c6e76f112e4ade) which removes the `trap "rm -rf '$TEMP_DIR'" EXIT` statement.

Assuming removing the `trap` was intentional (it may not have been; I can't find an issue or commit that refers specifically to that change, and it also temporarily disappeared in the "bad" pip8 branch merge), here's where the problem is:

```sh
if [ "$NO_SELF_UPGRADE" != 1 ]; then
  echo "Checking for new version..."
  TEMP_DIR=$(TempDir)

  ...

  if ! REMOTE_VERSION=`"$LE_PYTHON" "$TEMP_DIR/fetch.py" --latest-version` ; then
    echo "WARNING: unable to check for updates."
  elif [ "$LE_AUTO_VERSION" != "$REMOTE_VERSION" ]; then

    ...

    # TODO: Clean up temp dir safely, even if it has quotes in its path.
    rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
  fi  # A newer version is available.

  >>>$TEMP_DIR cleanup should be here instead<<<
fi  # Self-upgrading is allowed.
```

issue_title: Make acme tests work with older versions of pytest
issue_body:
The acme package now relies on pytest when running tests through setuptools / `python setup.py test` and after the release we found out it has some issues when running tests with pytest < 3.0. The issues show up as errors talking about “import file mismatch” and can be fixed by adding a pytest.ini file with contents like:

```
[pytest]
norecursedirs = *.egg dist build
```

This pytest.ini file needs to be included in the acme package.

issue_title: Change GitHub URLs from mblayman to python-tap
per-scriptworker exit code config
issue_body:
In [this commit](https://github.com/mozilla-releng/scriptworker/commit/513fe573feb5eb4b6f9c4780b44586a057d85ebc), I allowed for an exit code of -11 to cause the task to retry, via the `intermittent-task` taskcluster status. This is global across all scriptworker instance types, even if we spin up a scriptworker instance that doesn't use python.

We should add a config dict that we use to update the `REVERSED_STATUSES`, so we can set the various exit code mapping per scriptworker instance type. That probably means `REVERSED_STATUSES` (and possibly `STATUSES`) will move into config.

(`STATUSES` could actually have a tuple of exit codes as values instead of an int... not decided about this.)

We could also ad a `default` value. Right now if we hit an unknown exit code, we specify failure. If we had a per-scriptworker-instance-type default value, we could tweak behavior on unknown exit codes.

issue_title: REST API updates
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

We need to update some parts of the Rest API for the new smart contracts. (ref #1383 )

- [x] `GET /api/<version>/address`: No changes needed
- [ ] `PUT /api/<version>/tokens/<token_address>`: Should return `token_network_address` instead of `channel_manager_address`
- [ ] `GET /api/<version>/channels/<channel_address>`: `channel_address` is a hash now, input checking will need to be changed
  - [ ] Should probably return the `token_network_address` as well
- [ ] `GET /api/<version>/channels`: Should probably return the `token_network_address` for each channel (see above)
- [x] `GET /api/<version>/tokens`: No changes needed
- [x] `GET /api/<version>/tokens/<token_address>/partners`: No changes needed
- [ ] `PUT /api/<version>/token_swaps/<target_address>/<identifier>`: Should be removed for red eyes
- [x] `PUT /api/<version>/channels`: No changes needed
- [ ] `PATCH /api/<version>/channels/<channel_address>`: `channel_address` is a hash now, input checking will need to be changed
- [x] `PUT /api/<version>/connections/<token_address>`: No changes needed
- [x] `DELETE /api/<version>/connections/<token_address>`: No changes needed
- [x] `GET /api/<version>/connections`: No changes needed
- [x] `POST /api/<version>/transfers/<token_address>/<target_address>`: No changes needed
- [ ] `GET /api/<version>/events/network`
  - [ ] Should return `token_network_address` instead of `channel_manager_address`
- [ ] `GET /api/<version>/events/tokens/<token_address>`
  - [ ] Should return `channel_identifier` instead of `netting_channel`
- [ ] `GET /api/<version>/events/channels/<channel_registry_address>`: Not called `channel_registry_address`. Should be `token_network_address`.

issue_title: Inserting into table with ForeignKey
issue_body:
Hello! I have a problem which is probably trivial but I cannot figure out what is troubling me. I am trying to insert some data into table which has defined foreign key to some other table:

```
class Users(CommonColumns):
    __tablename__ = 'vb_users'
    __table_args__ = {'schema': 'test'}

    id = Column(BigInteger, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    user_name = Column(Text, nullable=False, unique=True)
    api_key = Column(Text, nullable=False, unique=True)
    api_secret = Column(Text, nullable=False, unique=True)

class DataIn(CommonColumns):
    __tablename__ = 'vb_data_in'
    __table_args__ = {'schema': 'test'}

    id = Column(BigInteger, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    user_id = Column(ForeignKey('test.vb_users.id', ondelete='RESTRICT',
                                onupdate='CASCADE'), nullable=False)
    user_data = Column(JSON, nullable=False)
    request_session = Column(Text)

    user = relationship(Users)
```

Relevant part of my `settings.py`:

```
ID_FIELD = 'id'
ITEM_LOOKUP_FIELD = ID_FIELD
config.ID_FIELD = ID_FIELD
config.ITEM_LOOKUP_FIELD = ID_FIELD

# Need to be called *after* setting config.ID_FIELD
registerSchema('users')(Users)
registerSchema('datain')(DataIn)

DOMAIN = {
    'users': Users._eve_schema['users'],
    'datain': DataIn._eve_schema['datain'],
}

DOMAIN['users'].update({
    'description': 'Get user info.',
    'id_field': 'id',
    'item_lookup_field': 'id',
})

DOMAIN['datain'].update({
    'description': 'Endpoint for inserting data.',
    'id_field': 'id',
    'item_lookup_field': 'id',
    'resource_methods': ['POST'],
})
```

I am also using HMAC authentication class where I select user from database and return `True` if everything went fine.

When I try to insert something in my `DataIn` table, I get this error:
```
{
  "_error": {
    "code": 422,
    "message": "Insertion failure: 1 document(s) contain(s) error(s)"
  },
  "_issues": {
    "user": "required field"
  },
  "_status": "ERR"
}
```

This is not the only problem I have. There is also column `request_session` which should be inserted after some calculations in my code, not by user data.

My questions are:

- how to properly handle this simple relation? Should I assign `Users` object to Flask `app` after successful authentication or something similar?
- how can I insert `request_session` column, which should be calculated after each request? Can I also somehow assign it to my `app` so that Eve-SQLAlchemy becomes aware of it?

Thanks a lot for all your effort with this library!!! ;)

issue_title: python kubeconfig loading doesn't match kubectl config loading...
issue_body:
I had a .kube/config file with duplicate clusters with the same name (but different information) in the config file, the three different entries each pointed to different clusters, two were dead, but one was alive (I had be repeatedly creating and deleting clusters)

`kubectl` command line happened to chose the working one and worked correctly.

python client library chose the first one in the file (which wasn't working) and failed.

This lead to a very confused experience until I figured out what was going on.

It will be difficult to emulate the `kubectl` parsing exactly, but we should detect duplicate entries and throw an exception.  (and I would argue that `kubectl` probably should too)

This actually probably applies to all client libraries in different languages.

@mbohlool

issue_title: Why is stack info in findCaller limited to PY3?
issue_body:
In ABSLLogger.findCaller(), the documentation says:
```
stack_info: bool, when using Python 3 and True, include the stack trace as the fourth item returned instead of None.
```
https://github.com/abseil/abseil-py/blob/master/absl/logging/__init__.py#L885

First off, that's a bit confusing. It makes it sounds like it'll return None if you're using PY2, but it actually returns 3-tuple, making PY2-3 compatibility messy.

The bigger question though is why? I'm not sure what part of that code isn't compatible. Python 2 has StringIO and traceback.print_stack(), so why is stack information only done in PY3?

issue_title: S3 content-type download when upload content-type was specified is being ignored
issue_body:
According to S3 documentation (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectPUT.html) the content-type set when downloading a file from S3 is supposed to be:
1.  The same content-type provided when uploading the file;
2.  ``binary/octet-stream`` otherwise.

Using this test script (from @TheJefe) we can see that is not what is actually happening. 

```python
import boto3
import os
from botocore.config import Config
import requests

bucket_name = 'test'
filepath = 'blah.txt'
key = 'blah.txt'
host = 'localhost'
port = 4572

s3 = boto3.resource(
    's3',
    endpoint_url = 'http://{}:{}'.format(host, port)
)

s3.create_bucket(Bucket=bucket_name)
bucket = s3.Bucket(bucket_name)

bucket.upload_file(filepath, key, ExtraArgs={"ACL": 'public-read', "ContentType": 'text/html'})
url = s3.meta.client.generate_presigned_url('get_object', ExpiresIn=0, Params={'Bucket': bucket_name, 'Key': key})
url = url.split('?')[0]
response = requests.get(url)
print 'Response content-type for text/html: ' + response.headers['Content-Type']

bucket.delete_objects(Delete={'Objects': [{'Key': key},],})

bucket.upload_file(filepath, key, ExtraArgs={"ACL": 'public-read'3.create_bucket(Bucket='test-bucket'))
url = s3.meta.client.generate_presigned_url('get_object', ExpiresIn=0, Params={'Bucket': bucket_name, 'Key': key})
url = url.split('?')[0]
response = requests.get(url)
print 'Response content-type for binary/octet-stream: ' + response.headers['Content-Type']
```

After #565 things change a little bit and all download content-types are set to ``binary/octet-stream``, independent from the provided content-type in the upload step. Before that, version 0.8.4, the content-type seems to be inferred from the uploaded archive when it wasn't provided in the upload step, otherwise it is correct. The result from the previous script agaisnt 0.8.4 is the following:
```text
Response content-type for text/html: text/html
Response content-type for binary/octet-stream: text/html; charset=utf-8
```

    'h2': [hit / self.tree_index.nodename_to_kmercount[nodename]],
ZeroDivisionError: division by zero
```
I believe this could happen even with the standard parameters, and it just becomes more probable with the k-mer level LCA, since the k-mer count of the resulting node is more likely to be 0. It should therefore be fixed ASAP.

What do you think we should output as `h2` when the k-mer count is 0?

issue_title: Literal init with both language and datatype
issue_body:
kyleheus...@gmail.com, 2009-08-27T22:53:49.000Z 
When you initialize a Literal type with both a language and datatype, only 
datatype is set (language value is discarded and set to None)

But if you manually set a literal object's language attribute, it sticks 
regardless of whether datatype was set.

When you serialize or call the literal's n3() method, it is possible to 
output both language and datatype.

So, the question is, why can't a Literal be initialized with both langauge 
and datatype (ie. via the __new__ method)?

ex.1
>> l = Literal(u'hello',u'en-us',XSD_NS['string'])
>> l.language
None
>> l.n3()
u'"hello"^^'

ex.2
>> l = Literal(u'hello',XSD_NS['string'])
>> l.language = u'en-us'
>> l.n3()
u'"hello"@en-us^^'

Comment 1 by lindstr...@gmail.com
In RDF literal values cannot have both a language and a datatype. It's either or or neither. Thus this should 
reasonably not be changed.

(It *is* possible right now to get those strange serializations by manipulating a Literal instance though, but that 
may/should go away as well..)

I propose WontFix.


Comment 2 by eik...@gmail.com



Comment 3 by eik...@gmail.com



Comment 4 by drewpca
I propose, and I hope to write the patch tonight, that Literal raise a TypeError if 
both the args are passed in. Quietly ignoring one of the args isn't useful. Also, 
__new__ can have a docstring explaining this with a pointer to the spec, since clearly 
it's surprising people. 

In a related fix, Literal's __repr__ shouldn't ever emit datatype and language args, 
and it should not omit None args at all, just to save space.


Comment 5 by drewpca



Comment 6 by drewpca
Waiting for review: http://code.google.com/p/rdflib/source/branch?
spec=issue107&branch=/branches/literalnew-81


Comment 7 by drewpca
Merged, with a change to the rdf/xml parser, since an rdf/xml node CAN have a lang and 
datatype. (The lang is ignored, says http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-rdf-syntax/#section-
Syntax-languages)

issue_title: split socket.pyx into pyx/py
issue_body:
Only the basic methods of the socket need to be Cython:

* init
* close
* send
* recv
* getsockopt
* setsockopt

All the derivative and sugar methods (multipart, json, sockopts as attrs, etc.) do not need to be Cython, and do not benefit from being compiled.  I would like to split the Socket file into two: a minimal base class in Cython, and the extended methods on a subclass in Python.

This would reduce compile time, and significantly reduce the need to recompile when working on new features, as the core methods haven't been touched in quite some time.
